title: React Testing Library
summary: React Testing Library helps you test React components.
It checks what the user actually sees and does.
It pairs with a runner like Jest.

sections.text: Testing a React component is a bit different from testing a plain function. React Testing Library is the standard tool for it, and it pushes you toward testing your components the way a real user would experience them.

sections.title: Testing Like a User
sections.text: React Testing Library lets you render a component in a test and then interact with it as a user would, finding a button by its text and clicking it, checking that the right message appears. It focuses on what the user sees and does, not on the hidden internal workings.

sections.title: Why That Approach
sections.text: By testing what the user experiences, rather than internal details, your tests keep passing even when you refactor the inside of a component, as long as it still behaves the same for the user. This makes tests less brittle and more meaningful. It pairs with a runner like Jest, which actually runs the tests.

media.alt: A component tested by finding and clicking elements as a user would

sections.title: Reviewing a Restaurant by Eating There
sections.text: Testing internal details is like judging a restaurant by inspecting its kitchen layout. React Testing Library is like judging it by actually eating the meal, the way a customer does. As long as the food is great, it does not matter how they rearranged the kitchen. You test the experience, not the wiring.

media.alt: A diner judging a restaurant by the meal, not the kitchen layout

sections.title: Using React Testing Library
items: Render the component in a test.
Find elements the way a user would, like by text.
Interact with them, like clicking a button.
Check the user visible result is correct.
Let a runner like Jest run the test.

sections.text: This library's guiding motto is that the more your tests resemble how the software is used, the more confidence they give you. That single idea shapes its whole design.

terms.definition: A tool for testing React components.
Whose experience the tests focus on.
Drawing the component inside a test.
The runner that actually runs the tests.

questions.explanation: React Testing Library helps you test React components.
